extraction.one_line_summary: VINE Jay won Tour Down Under by 16 seconds over YATES Simon after Yates won stage 5 but couldn't close the gap.
tactical_patterns: HEPBURN stayed in breakaway to pace YATES rather than chasing stage, demonstrating controlled team support over individual glory.
INEOS hesitated closing gap to CATTANEO knowing his TT strength, revealing GC teams fear strong time trialists in breaks.
YATES attacked with 3km remaining only after breakaway absorbed, showing reluctance to commit until forced by race situation.
HAYTER mechanical at back of peloton but immediately back at front shows INEOS depth compensating for positional errors.

rider_signals.VINE Jay: positive: won overall without winning stage, showing superior consistency and time trial prowess across multi-day race
rider_signals.YATES Simon: neutral: stage win but 16-second overall deficit suggests sprint finish doesn't suit his attacking style for GC gains
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: dropped 2 seconds in sprint finish and slipped overall, revealing sprint weakness despite climbing strength
rider_signals.CATTANEO Mattia: positive: forced entire peloton chase from INEOS due to TT reputation, showing threat perception exceeds current form
rider_signals.HAYTER Ethan: negative: mechanical while positioned at back suggests poor race craft for critical final stage
rider_signals.TIBERI Antonio: positive: moved into 8th overall via late attack, showing opportunistic GC gains when favorites marked each other
